name: pr_tms_selfloop_strict
drl:
rule RM when Ord(v > 0) then insertLogical(new M1(0)); end
rule ESC when M1(n < 2) then insertLogical(new M1(2)); end
_finding: The STRICT-PROGRESS self-loop (round 18, case C - the safe side of the plateau boundary): the insert M1(2) falls OUTSIDE the guard (n < 2), so nothing self-justifies; deleting the root cascades the whole chain (M1(0) then M1(2)) to empty in both engines. The pair with pr_tms_selfloop_plateau pins the satisfiability boundary the authoring lint decides statically.
